name: The Gevasci's Ricotta Tart
recipeInstructions:
1. Ensure all ingredients are at room temperature before starting.
2. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
3. Mix the first measure of flour with baking powder and salt in a bowl.
4. Using your hands, work in the softened butter until it resembles breadcrumbs.
5. Gradually add the brandy, mixing lightly until the dough just holds together.
6. Chill the dough for about 30 minutes.
7. Roll out about two-thirds of the chilled dough to a thickness of 1/8 inch and line a (10-inch) cake pan with it.
8. In another bowl, mix the second measure of flour with ricotta, chopped almonds, pine nuts, and angelica or citron until combined.
9. Beat the eggs until light and lemon-colored.
10. Gradually beat in the sugar, then add vanilla and stir into the cheese mixture.
11. Pour the mixture into the lined cake pan(s).
12. Roll out the remaining pastry to a thickness of 1/8 inch, cut into strips, and arrange them in a lattice pattern on top of the tart(s).
13. Bake until firm for about 40 minutes.
14. Cool before serving. Sift confectioners' sugar lightly over the top just before serving.
